page: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. System memory consists of 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the m6i.2xlarge consistently in the average tier across most standard workloads. It demonstrates stable execution in single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests, as well as standard memory bandwidth operations, reaching 413.91 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 58.41 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks fall into the bottom 25% performance tier, indicating a performance bottleneck for memory-intensive decompression and larger model execution.

The instance offers a balanced resource profile, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or high-speed local storage. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is appropriate for standard web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It is designed for application servers, containerized microservices, and moderate Redis caching layers, but is less effective for large-scale LLM inference or heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmark data positions this instance in the average tier for most compute, memory, and database tasks, though it exhibits lower performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium-parameter LLM inference. With a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and support for advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, the m6i.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard workloads. It is appropriate for general-purpose applications, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3 Gbps baseline network speed, optimized for balanced workloads on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR4 memory for stable, mid-tier compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
32.0 GB of DDR4 system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average performance across standard database, web serving, and compilation benchmarks
Hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector instructions including AVX-512
Appropriate for general-purpose applications, web servers, and moderate database tasks
